props.caption: Eleven of eleven: the airframe that ended Russia's rear area reaches Omsk

items.note: Nineteen days after signing the Islamabad memorandum, Iran's forces hit two tankers at the strait's mouth — a Qatari LNG carrier and, per maritime-security sources, a Saudi crude ship. US officials told Axios retaliation is likely; talks resume after the 9 July burial.

Ukraine's drones crossed roughly 2,500 kilometres to hit Russia's largest refinery, the last of its eleven biggest gasoline plants yet unstruck. The same night Kyiv says it hit eight shadow-fleet tankers supplying Crimea.

items.place: Paris · the bracelet and the ballot
items.note: The appeals court upheld Le Pen's conviction, cut her ineligibility to a term that leaves 2027 formally open, and ordered a year under an electronic tag she has said she could not campaign in. Bardella talk started within hours.

OPEC+'s fifth straight monthly addition met Iran's missiles inside twenty-four hours: Brent settled at $71.99 on the glut, then bounced to $72.60 on the strait. The desk's sub-$70 call is owned at p=0.25 and settles Friday.

items.what: NATO's summit opened with a draft pledging an ironclad Article 5, €70bn for Ukraine in 2026, a new A400M strategic airlift fleet, and Korea's president in the room under the Indo-Pacific partner format.

Belgium beat the United States 4-1 and the hosts are out; the striker FIFA cleared under Article 27 changed nothing on the pitch. FIFA ruled Belgium's internal challenge inadmissible and UEFA published a red-line statement — the CAS deadline the desk priced lands Wednesday.
Search-and-rescue at the struck residential block concluded at nineteen dead including a child, with sixty-one injured, per Ukraine's strategic communications office.
Malian army channels claim FLA leader Hamza Ag Atlal died in the 4 July fighting near Anefis; the claim is one side's war reporting and remains unverified.
Venezuela's quake toll rose again to 3,535 dead with thousands still displaced, as the recovery militarizes around La Guaira.
The DRC's outbreak reached 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths, and the first imported case in France sharpened WHO's warnings on health-worker infections.
